---
title: "AI-Assisted Clinical Problem Solving"
subtitle: "A Multi-Persona Bayesian Diagnostic Reasoning Framework for Medical Education"
author:
  - name: Hsieh-Ting Lin
    affiliations:
      - Placeholder University Hospital, Taipei, Taiwan
format:
  typst:
    papersize: a0
    columns: 3
    margin:
      x: 2cm
      y: 2cm
bibliography: ../shared/references.bib
---

# Background

Clinical diagnostic reasoning demands integration of multi-disciplinary expertise and systematic evaluation of diagnostic tests. We developed an AI-assisted **Clinical Problem Solving (CPS)** framework that models the NEJM Clinical Problem-Solving format using multi-persona Bayesian reasoning with likelihood ratios [@mcgee2018; @simel2009].

# Framework Architecture

**SCENARIO** &rarr; Symptom Mapping &rarr; **Round 1** (Attending) &rarr; **Round 2** (Radiology + Pathology) &rarr; **Round 3** (Subspecialist) &rarr; **Round 4** (EBM Evidence) &rarr; **Round 5** (Conference) &rarr; **FINAL_DX**

## Eight Medical Personas

| Persona | Role | Trigger |
|---------|------|---------|
| Attending (IM) | H&P, initial DDx | Always |
| Radiologist | Imaging interpretation | Imaging data |
| Pathologist | Lab/biopsy interpretation | Lab data |
| Cardiologist | ECG, echo, cath | Chest pain, syncope |
| Pulmonologist | PFTs, ABG | Dyspnea, wheezing |
| Infectious Disease | Cultures, serologies | Fever |
| Neurologist | Neuro exam, imaging | Headache, AMS |
| EBM Specialist | Literature search | Always |

## Bayesian LR Methodology

$$\text{Post-test odds} = \text{Pre-test odds} \times LR$$

$$\text{Post-test probability} = \frac{\text{Post-test odds}}{1 + \text{Post-test odds}}$$

Sequential LR application creates a transparent probability cascade for each diagnosis [@fagan1975].

# Case 1: Lambert-Eaton Myasthenic Syndrome

**76F**: 6-month progressive proximal weakness, bilateral ptosis, areflexia, respiratory failure, prior SIADH.

| Finding | LR | Pre | Post |
|---------|-----|-----|------|
| Proximal weakness + areflexia | x5.0 | 35.0% | 72.9% |
| Low/absent CMAPs | x8.0 | 72.9% | 95.6% |
| SIADH (autonomic) | x3.0 | 95.6% | 98.5% |
| Failed MG therapy | x4.0 | 98.5% | **99.6%** |

**Final Dx**: LEMS, paraneoplastic from SCLC. Correctly identified by Round 4.

# Case 2: NF1 Vasculopathy --- Five Diagnostic Pivots

**38F**: Mail carrier, prior MI at <38, sudden chest pain, NTG non-responsive.

| Pivot | New Data | Dx | Prob |
|-------|----------|-----|------|
| 1 | Young F + prior MI | SCAD | 97.5% |
| 2 | Multi-territory MI | APS | 94.7% |
| 3 | 3-vessel aneurysms | Kawasaki | 90% |
| 4 | Skin papules | KD/NF1 | 50/10% |
| 5 | CAL spots + freckling | **NF1** | **>95%** |

**Key lesson**: Bayesian reasoning only works within the hypothesis space you define. NF1 was not in the initial Top 10.

# Validation Module

## Six Safety Checks (Derived from Case Failures)

| Check | Trigger | Grade Impact |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| Red Flag History | Unusual PMH age | D &rarr; B |
| Syndromic Screen | Young + unclear etiology | C+ &rarr; A |
| Rare Cause Search | Unexpected pattern | B- &rarr; A |
| Hypothesis Space Audit | After every round | Mandatory |
| Genetic Pattern Recognition | AD family history | B+ &rarr; A |
| Hypothesis Humility | Always | Principle |

## Retrospective Grading (Case 2)

| Phase | Grade | Failure Mode |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| 1: Case Intake | D | Anchoring on SCAD |
| 2: DDx Generation | C+ | No syndromic screen |
| 3: Diagnostic Testing | B- | NF1 not in knowledge base |
| 4: Subspecialty | B+ | NF1 underweighted |
| 5: Final Dx | A | Instant recognition |

# Performance Metrics

| Metric | Case 1 | Case 2 |
|--------|--------|--------|
| Diagnostic Accuracy | 1.0 | 1.0 |
| Pivot Efficiency | Round 1 | Round 5 |
| MNM Sensitivity | 100% | 100% |
| Hypothesis Coverage | Yes (R1) | No (R4) |
| LR Completeness | 85% | 78% |

# Conclusions

1. AI-assisted CPS provides **transparent, quantifiable** diagnostic reasoning with LR-based probability cascades
2. The **validation-retrospective cycle** converts case failures into safety checks, enabling continuous improvement
3. **Bayesian reasoning is bounded by the hypothesis space** --- the most important pedagogical insight for teaching DDx construction
4. Multi-persona rounds with subspecialty activation ensure **comprehensive** case analysis

# References
